methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Gauss Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised €9M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only one same-stage comparable and one adjacent-stage peer, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable triangulation. Expanding to all eight same-sector peers would introduce stage and scale heterogeneity that would compromise valuation accuracy. A cross-sector median would misrepresent nuclear technology risk and capital intensity characteristics specific to frontier fission and fusion subsegments.

Without a sufficiently dense peer cohort at Series A in nuclear energy, traditional comparable-company analysis cannot be meaningfully applied. Valuation support for Gauss Fusion would require alternative methodologies—such as discounted cash flow analysis tied to regulatory and technical milestones, venture capital method benchmarking against private round pricing, or staged-funding analysis within the nuclear subsegment. The primary uncertainty is the absence of reliable market pricing for early-stage nuclear technology ventures, a structural feature of this emerging sector rather than a data limitation.
